text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, nestled beside a peaceful brook was a little foxden. Within the cosy den lived Stella the squirrel. Stella loved sunrays peeking through the leaves.

One serene day, Stella decided she wanted something special. With tiny mouse fingers clutching a shiny beetle shell she'd found under an old oak leaf, she danced all afternoon. Her tail flicks made gentle music as she imagined becoming a beauty like those glossy owls.

As night whispers sounded sweetly around her cozy home, Stella tucked her prize snugly into a tight pocket. Perhaps she could exhibit her beauty? Perhaps feathers?

She dreamed till dawn. Stella was famous. She might adorn every twig. Whispers turned into shouts.

But then… an unexpected shadow.

Fangs. Cold grey. Birds faltered. It was big... Very big.

Stella didn't think.

It flew away? Into the cityscape?

Or maybe she wouldn't want anyone?

She wouldn't want cats…
